name: Raspberry-Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lbs semi-sweet chocolate (I use chocolate chip)
20 drops raspberry candy concentrate (available in specialty stores.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat the oven to 375 degrees.
2. For the crust: Lightly spray the spring-form pan with a non-stick spray such as PAM. Mix graham crackers and sugar in a bowl and dust the pan.
3. For the filling: Soften the cream cheese by microwaving in a bowl for about 30 seconds per half-pound (2 - 2 1/2 minutes). Add two eggs and mix with an electric mixer until well incorporated. Slowly add the sugar, about 1/2 cup at a time, until incorporated. Add the remaining two eggs and mix until there are no more lumps and the batter is smooth (about three minutes).
4. Melt the chocolate in a separate container. This could be done in the microwave or over the stove. Do not scorch the bottom as it will make the chocolate taste bitter and harder to clean.
5. Add the melted chocolate to the batter and mix on high. While mixing, add the raspberry candy concentrate with an eye-dropper. Experiment with this step; the more concentrate added, the stronger the flavor. Be careful not to overdo it, as it can make the cheesecake taste like cough syrup.
6. Pour the mixture into the spring-form pan and bake until the center is set (The top of the cheesecake will be glossy while baking. When the luster of the center begins to dull, it is ready.) The cheesecake may still feel "jiggly" (like jello). That's okay; it will set in the refrigerator.
7. Allow the cheesecake to cool enough to handle the pan without oven mitts and place in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours. This dish is best prepared a day or two in advance.
